2605.06749.title: A Statistical Framework for Algorithmic Collective Action with Multiple Collectives
2605.06749.paper_summary: As learning systems increasingly shape everyday decisions, Algorithmic Collective Action (ACA), i.e., users coordinating changes to shared data to steer model behavior, offers a complement to regulator-side policy and corporate model design. Real-world collective actions have traditionally been decentralized and fragmented into multiple collectives, despite sharing overarching objectives, with each collective differing in size, strategy, and actionable goals. However, most of the ACA literature focuses on single collective settings. To address this, we propose the first comprehensive statistical framework for ACA with multiple collectives acting on the same system. In particular, we focus on collective action in classification, studying how multiple collectives can influence a classifier's behavior. We provide quantitative statistical bounds on the success of the collectives, considering the role and the interplay of the collectives' sizes and the alignment of their goals. We make such bounds computable by each collective with only partial knowledge of other collectives' sizes and strategies. Finally, we numerically illustrate our framework on simulations inspired by interventions for climate adaptation in smart cities, demonstrating the usefulness of our bounds.
2605.06749.paper_summary_zh: 隨著學習系統越來越多地影響日常決策，算法集體行動（ACA），即用戶協調對共享數據的變更以引導模型行為，為監管方政策和企業模型設計提供了一種補充。儘管擁有共同的目標，現實世界中的集體行動傳統上是去中心化的，並且分散為多個集體，每個集體在規模、策略和可行目標上各不相同。然而，大多數ACA文獻集中於單一集體的情境。為了解決這個問題，我們提出了首個針對多個集體在同一系統上進行行動的ACA的綜合統計框架。特別是，我們專注於分類中的集體行動，研究多個集體如何影響分類器的行為。我們提供了集體成功的定量統計界限，考慮到集體的規模及其目標的對齊之間的角色和相互作用。我們使這些界限可由每個集體計算，僅需對其他集體的規模和策略有部分了解。最後，我們在受氣候適應介入啟發的智慧城市模擬中數值展示了我們的框架，證明了我們界限的實用性。
2605.06749.author: Claudio Battiloro et.al.
2605.06749.authors: Claudio Battiloro, Pietro Greiner, Dario Rancati, Bret Nestor, Oumaima Amezgar, Francesca Dominici
